breadcrumbs.id: linux artifacts & forensic evidence collection/user and privilege artifacts
breadcrumbs.title: User and Privilege Artifacts

overview: The /etc/passwd file stores essential information about user accounts on a Linux system. It is a text file where each line represents a user account, with fields separated by colons (:). Despite the name, this file does not contain actual passwords—it contains placeholders and metadata.

study.overview: The /etc/passwd file stores essential information about user accounts on a Linux system. It is a text file where each line represents a user account, with fields separated by colons (:). Despite the name, this file does not contain actual passwords—it contains placeholders and metadata.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
